action.content: Write an email to your friend about what triggers you. Make sure to wrap the entire email in double quotation marks.
action.label: context

action.content:
"Hey Alex,

I hope you're doing well! I've been reflecting a lot lately on what sets me off, and I thought I'd share it with you since you know me so well.

Honestly, the biggest trigger for me is feeling unheard or dismissed. When someone interrupts me mid-sentence or rolls their eyes while I'm explaining my perspective, it makes me feel like my thoughts don't matter. It instantly puts me on the defensive, and I end up arguing just to be validated.

Another big one is unpredictability. I thrive on routine and preparation, so when plans change at the last minute without any warning, I get really anxious and frustrated. It feels like a lack of respect for my time and effort.

I'm sharing this not to make you feel bad, but because I value our friendship and want to communicate openly. If I ever seem short-tempered, it's usually one of these things happening in the background. Thanks for listening!

Best,
Jamie"
action.label: response
